rumors.text: Bobby Krivitsky: A piece of advice Amile Jefferson got from the Celtics ’ recent Summer League head coaches was to experiment with conviction. I asked Jefferson if there were some examples of that he could share. “We have a ton of amazing coaches, and they're all really creative. And so we put in a bunch of different things, game plan-wise, and we taught these guys a bunch of new techniques for stealing the ball, for…being defensive playmakers, and that's in a bunch of different ways . “And that's in practice, and then we try to implement it in the game. And then offensively, having coordinated attacks. And one of those things is like transition, not just talking about transition, but actually putting a game plan in for transition, things like that. “So just trying to be creative and find different ways to get things on film that maybe could help us during the season.”

Bobby Krivitsky: I asked Amile Jefferson if he’s getting feedback from people like Joe Mazzulla, Jon Scheyer, and Coach K during his opportunity as the Celtics ’ Summer League head coach. “All those people you named are really in my corner and have been super helpful to me in my career. “Jon's one of my closest friends. He's really smart. He's an amazing coach. So as much as I can talk to him; the feedback that I get from Joe is always really good, and he's been amazing to my growth and my career . “So especially those two, being able to talk to them before games, after games, just making sure I'm good and knowing that I have their support is really helpful to me.”

Shams Charania: The Los Angeles Lakers are promoting South Bay coach Zach Guthrie to a Lakers assistant coach and head of player development on JJ Redick's staff, sources tell ESPN. Guthrie guided South Bay to the G League's best record last season.

Nathan Bubes walked into the makeshift locker room at halftime of the Minnesota Timberwolves ’ Las Vegas Summer League opener against New Orleans with a message. “I told them we need to take a deep breath,” Bubes said. “And as I said that, I was like, ‘So do I.'” Summer league is a chance for players with a lower profile to get noticed and try to earn a spot on a roster, either as a full-time or two-way player. It also offers that opportunity to young coaches, who are typically entrusted with running games and practices as they try to work their way up the ladder. This is the chance Bubes has been dreaming about since he entered the NBA as a barely paid intern with the Los Angeles Lakers in 2018. Long encouraged by those around him to go into the front-office side of basketball operations, Bubes has been determined to make his way in coaching. Now, as the leader of the Wolves’ summer team, he is charged with furthering the development of Joan Beringer and Rocco Zikarsky, getting second-round draft pick Isaiah Evans up to speed and helping to evaluate other players, including promising point guard Zyon Pullin, two-way forward Enrique Freeman and sharpshooter Nate Santos.

Bubes has long been the right-hand man for Wolves head coach Chris Finch, winning him over when both were in New Orleans and then serving as his chief of staff in Minnesota. He’s done everything asked of him, including serving as the man on the hot seat during games to determine if Finch should challenge a call, spending a season as an assistant with the G League team in Iowa and easing Finch’s day-to-day operations by helping with planning, organizing and communicating with the rest of the staff. “He deserves the opportunity just because of how important he’s been to me,” Finch said. “So, I figured this was the summer to do that.”

Denver is seen as an increasingly likely landing spot for former Grizzlies and Kings coach Dave Joerger . The Stein Line first reported in late June that Joerger had interviewed with the Nuggets in their search for a more experienced top assistant to head coach David Adelman.
Justin Martinez: Mark Daigneault has officially been named an assistant coach for the USA Basketball men’s national team

Brunson, on hand with his wife, Ali Marks Brunson, accepted his award on the David H. Koch Theater stage while dressed to the nines in a tuxedo. “First and foremost, I want to thank my family for sacrificing everything day in and day out,” Brunson said. “Thank you to [owner] Jim Dolan and the Knicks organization, [president] Leon [Rose], the front office, Mike Brown, my teammates — without them, none of this would be possible; they allow me to be me. I’m forever indebted to them.”

Jason Beede: #Magic summer league coach D.J. Bakker told @Josh_Cohen_NBA he was impressed with Jase Richardson’s ability to control the tempo of the game: “I told him, even in the late game … I want the ball in his hands,” Bakker said. “Get us organized.”

Mike Budenholzer was a coach who believed in routine and a process of team building that had proved successful. A big part of that were his “break breads,” which were team dinners or gatherings, win or lose, on the road . “Break bread after every game – after every game – every game that we played that we stayed over there was a place for all of the players and all of the families to eat,” Giannis Antetokounmpo said. “And it was always packed. Everybody went. Because we knew it was part of our culture. Everybody had to show face. [With] all these things, there's a lot of owners that are like, 'you're spending money,' the food budget and all that goes up. Like a million dollars to $2.5 so they might not like it. But in order for you to win, you gotta spend money.”

Marc Lasry: “I think COVID, what it did was, it could either split apart a team or it brings everybody together. Bud did a great job of keeping everybody together. We talked about it. Look, ‘We need to do this. We’re going to try to do team dinners, we’re going to try to do all these different things so the team could feel we’re all in this together.’ I think that ended up working well. I think Bud did a great job, but I also think the players understood what it meant and the players bought into it.“

Mike Budenholzer: “That team was ready. They had what it took. So I think you just hopefully create an environment and space where they can be their best. All those decisions, all the things we did to create culture, just let them know we care about them and put them in a position to be great. And I'm very appreciative that those guys – I think they do. They all love to talk about it. I think there's a lot of fond memories and appreciation. That's the stuff I love. I love the games, I love winning and all that, but the stuff away from the court is just as important and just as meaningful.”

Michael Scotto: Sources: The Chicago Bulls will add Rex Kalamian, Jonah Herscu, and Blake Ahearn as assistant coaches . Kalamian was an assistant coach for the Bucks, Pistons, Kings, Clippers, Raptors, Thunder, Timberwolves, and Nuggets. Herscu was Portland’s G League head coach and assistant coach for the Blazers and Kings. Ahearn was a Grizzlies assistant coach and San Antonio’s G League head coach.

Udonis Haslem: First of all Bam is 28. Tyler is 26. Neither one of them got it all figured out yet. Maybe I was giving you too much credit at the time cause I thought you did so that’s why I commented. 2nd, anyone who knows bam knows he’s strong as baby bear. If he would’ve unloaded on Tyler 100% it’s over. You fired off on that young boy like it was a club punch and you never met him before in your life. 3rd yeah I did go at Jimmy. You damn right. The shit you pull in golden state and that shit he pulled in Chicago talking crazy to Hoiberg and disrespecting teammates in practice in Minnesota ain’t gon fly in the 305. So yeah. I checked his ass .

Michael Scotto: The Golden State Warriors are hiring Frank Vogel as their lead assistant coach, league sources told @hoopshype. Vogel spent last season as an assistant coach with the Dallas Mavericks . Vogel won the 2020 NBA championship with the Los Angeles Lakers as their head coach.
Sean Highkin: Tom Dundon is here for the Blazers' second game of Summer League, sitting courtside with Joe Cronin and Micah Nori.

James Plowright: Charles Lee on the off-season trades "Our front office made some moves, I thought they had the long term vision insight... I know some of the moves seem daunting to them (The fans) but it's going to help us in the long run."
James Plowright: Charles Lee on if anyone has surprised him "I think our two two-way guys have done a great job out here, Michael (Ajayi) and Kylan (Boswell)"
Congratulations to Coach Bob McAdoo on being awarded the 2026 Tex Winter Assistant Coach Lifetime Impact Award! 🤝

Bob McAdoo, a longtime NBA Assistant Coach and a member of the Naismith Memorial Basketball Hall of Fame, is the recipient of the 2026 Tex Winter Assistant Coach Lifetime Impact Award, the National Basketball Coaches Association (NBCA) announced today. “Bob McAdoo is one of the true giants of our game,” said J.B. Bickerstaff, Detroit Pistons Head Coach and NBCA President. “A Hall of Fame player, a champion, and an exceptional Coach, Bob has impacted generations of players through his knowledge, humility, and passion for teaching. His influence can be seen in the many players, coaches, and the Heat organization that he helped elevate throughout his coaching career. Congratulations to Coach McAdoo on this well-deserved recognition.”

“I am grateful, honored and humbled to have been selected by the NBCA Selection Committee for the 2026 Tex Winter Assistant Coach Lifetime Impact Award,” said Coach Bob McAdoo. “One never receives this type of award by oneself. I’d like to thank my wife Patrizia and family for all their love and support. I will be forever grateful to Pat Riley who took me on as an assistant coach for a fantastic NBA journey together. Thanks also to the Arison Family and the HEAT organization who have made an impact on my life both professionally and personally.”

"How much of his style helped you prepare for what you are about to embark upon?" Micah Nori: "The one thing that coach Finch is, he is very very good at what he does. Number one, there's no paranoia with him and the other thing is he will let you coach and gives you a voice. When those things happen, I was fortunate to be with him for a long time and work with him. Philosophy-wise and the way we think about basketball is very very similar."
Brad Rowland: After Antonio Lang was the head coach in Utah, the Hawks are turning HC duties over to Sanjay Lumpkin in Vegas . First time as a Summer League HC for Lumpkin.

After the Hawks declined Kuminga’s $24.3 million team option on June 29, Lakers president of basketball operations/general manager Rob Pelinka and coach JJ Redick met with Kuminga the following day. A part of the Lakers’ free-agency pitch to Kuminga, a source told the California Post, was the Lakers seeing Kuminga as a high-level, starting wing who would complete the vision of their roster reconstruction around superstar guard Luka Doncic .

Tony Parker and former teammate Patty Mills are together again with EuroLeague club LDLC ASVEL Villeurbanne. Parker recently took over as the team's head coach, while Mills signed with the French club this offseason.

Michael Grange: Raptors notes from Las Vegas: look for longtime NBA assistant Stephen Silas to join Darko Rajakovic’s staff shortly. Silas has worked for Don Nelson, Rick Carlisle & his father, Paul Silas in 25yr career. Former head coach w Rockets, recently coached Team USA in WC Qualifying.
Michael Grange: Former Raptors assistant Mike Batiste has taken a lead assistant job with his old club Panathinaikos in the EuroLeague. James Wade won’t be back with Raptors.

In the offseason, the Bucks replaced coach Doc Rivers with Taylor Jenkins. Yet Horst denied feeling any pressure that the team was forced to trade Antetokounmpo this summer. "This was not a 'have to,'" Horst said. "This was, we truly believed we've found an opportunity which is unique. We might be right. We might be wrong. But an opportunity where this is what's best for him and what he wants to pursue going forward, and this is what's best for us and what we want to pursue going forward. And that's why we made the decision.”
Giannis Antetokounmpo on his post-retirement plans: "Either I'm going to be a stay-at-home dad, or I'm going to be a coach. I want to be a coach in the NBA."

The Sacramento Kings and assistant coach Bobby Jackson are parting ways . Sources confirmed to KCRA 3's Sean Cunningham and Sports Director Michelle Dapper that the former King was leaving head coach Doug Christie's staff. He had been tasked with running the team's defense.

The Los Angeles Lakers continue to actively pursue unrestricted free agent wing Jonathan Kuminga, but they have not yet given him an enticing enough offer to commit, league sources told ESPN on Tuesday. After his $24.3 million team option was declined by the Atlanta Hawks on June 29, Kuminga met virtually the next day with Lakers general manager Rob Pelinka and coach JJ Redick, who pitched him on a vision of being a high-minutes wing next to Luka Doncic in a spacious on-court environment conducive to his skill set, league sources told ESPN.

The Cleveland Cavaliers also continue to have interest in Kuminga, league sources told ESPN. Kuminga has a high level of comfort with Cavaliers coach Kenny Atkinson, who coached Kuminga during his first three NBA seasons as a lead assistant for the Golden State Warriors. The Cavaliers , however, are currently wrapped in the LeBron James sweepstakes, leaving the Lakers as the most aggressive pursuer prior to the James domino falling.
